name: Seafood Chilaquiles Casserole
recipeIngredient: 10 Flour or Corn Tortillas (6-7 inches, cut into 1/2-inch strips)
1/2 cup Green Onions with tops, sliced
1 Canned Chipotle Chile in Adobo Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Tortillas should be 6 to 7 inches in diameter and cut into 1/2-inch strips.
2. Chipotle Chile should be a canned one in adobo sauce and finely chopped.
3. Bacon should be crisply cooked and crumbled.
Heat oil in a 10-inch skillet until hot. Cook tortilla strips in the hot oil until they are light golden brown, about 30 to 60 seconds, then drain. Set aside.
4. Cook onions in margarine in a 3-quart saucepan over low heat until tender.
5. Stir in flour, salt, and pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture is bubbly. Remove from heat.
6. Stir in Half & Half Light Cream. Heat to boiling, stirring constantly.
7. Boil and stir for 1 minute, then reduce the heat.
8. Stir in remaining ingredients except the bacon. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, just until the shrimp are pink (about 9 minutes).
9. Preheat oven to 350°F.
10. Layer half of the tortilla strips in the bottom of a greased 3-quart casserole dish.
11. Top with half of the seafood mixture.
12. Repeat with remaining tortilla strips and seafood mixture.
13. Top with bacon.
14. Bake until hot, 15 to 20 minutes.
